mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points over three months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in over 55 percent of instances when unemployment rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during expansion phases, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5 percent annually triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of months where CPI inflation exceeds 2.5 percent year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment yields a negative or zero 20-day forward return in more than 50 percent of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings potential, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward equity returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production greater than 0.5 percent signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening corporate output, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth falls below -0.5 percent
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in over 55 percent of instances following a monthly industrial production drop exceeding 0.5 percent.
